topic: A person discovers their partner has been taking secret phone calls
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment had become a thick, suffocating blanket. I was sorting through a pile of mail on the kitchen counter, the rhythmic crinkle of paper the only sound breaking the quiet. Then I noticed it—a faint, almost imperceptible vibration coming from the pocket of his jeans as he sat across the room on the sofa. My heart gave a sudden, hard thump against my ribs. I moved slowly, deliberately, approaching him. "Hey," I said, my voice sounding unnaturally level. He glanced up, a flicker of something guarded in his eyes, and quickly tucked his hand into his pocket. The air between us felt charged, brittle. I didn't press it. Instead, I picked up a book, pretending to be engrossed, but my gaze kept drifting back to that subtle tension in his posture, the way his jaw seemed a fraction too tight.

[story 2]

It started with the timing. He’d become meticulous about when he took his phone, always claiming an urgent work call. But lately, the calls weren't urgent; they were clandestine. One Tuesday evening, I overheard a snippet of laughter, a tone I didn't recognize, punctuated by hushed, rapid words. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. I watched him from the doorway, my breath catching in my throat. When the call ended, he seemed overly eager to resume his normal demeanor, a forced casualness that didn't quite reach his eyes. I retreated to the bathroom, gripping the cool porcelain sink until my knuckles were white. The sound of the running water was a flimsy shield against the churning feeling inside me.

[story 3]

I found the evidence tucked beneath a loose floorboard in his study—a small, burner phone, clearly out of place. My hands shook as I picked it up, the plastic cool against my palm. Every time I looked at the device, a wave of profound unease washed over me, making my vision swim slightly at the edges. I didn't confront him immediately. Instead, I spent the next hour pacing the length of the living room, the floorboards protesting beneath my bare feet. Each step felt heavy, weighted with unspoken questions. When he finally walked in, his face was open, relaxed, and utterly oblivious to the wreckage I was surveying. "What's wrong?" he asked, his tone warm, which only amplified the strange, hollow ache settling deep in my chest.
